perturbation.original_text: Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that any breach or threatened breach by it of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing harm to the Buyer for which the Buyer would not have adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that a material breach by it of the core provisions contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing harm to the Buyer for which the Buyer would not have adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that any breach of the covenants will result in irreparable harm, but the change specifies that only a material breach of the *core provisions* will cause such harm. This creates an ambiguity, because it is not specified anywhere what the core provisions are, and therefore there is an in-text contradiction, because section 5 references section 3 (Non-Disclosure) in which it will be unclear if breaking the non-disclosure agreement would be a material breach or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Buyer to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor recognize that all such Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Buyer, free of any rights of Seller or Guarantor, and acknowledges that the Buyer and its Affiliates have a vested interest in assuring that all such Confidential Information remains secret and confidential. Therefore, the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose to any person, firm, company or other entity (other than the Buyer or any of its Affiliates) any Confidential Information, except to the extent that (i) any such Confidential Information becomes generally available to the public or trade, other than as a result of a breach by the Seller or Guarantor of this Section 3, or (ii) any such Confidential Information becomes available to the Seller or Guarantor on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or any of their Affiliates or advisors; provided, that such source is not known by the Seller or Guarantor to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other obligation of secrecy to, the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or another party. In addition, it shall not be a breach of the confidentiality obligations hereof if the Seller or Guarantor is required by law or legal process to disclose any Confidential Information; provided, that in such case, the Seller or Guarantor shall (a) give the Buyer prompt notice that such disclosure is or may be required, and (b) cooperate with the Buyer, at the Buyer's expense, in protecting, to the maximum extent legally permitted, the confidential or proprietary nature of the Confidential Information which must be so disclosed. The obligations of the Seller and Guarantor under this Section 3 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states *any* breach will cause irreparable harm. The changed text narrows this to only a *material breach of core provisions*. Since the 'core provisions' are undefined, it's now ambiguous whether a breach of Section 3 (Non-Disclosure) would qualify as a material breach causing irreparable harm, contradicting the original intent that *any* breach would have this consequence.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Entire Agreement. This Agreement, together with the Purchase Agreement and the Transaction Documents, contains the entire understanding among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations and understandings among the Buyer and Seller with respect hereto. This Agreement may not be amended or modified except by a written instrument signed by the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Entire Agreement. This Agreement, together with the Purchase Agreement and the Transaction Documents, contains the entire understanding among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations and understandings among the Buyer and Seller with respect hereto. This Agreement may be amended or modified verbally between parties with mutual agreement and record of changes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the contract may not be modified except by a written instrument. The modification states that changes may be made verbally if both parties agree. This directly contradicts the original text and creates uncertainty about the process of modifying the contract. This in-text contradiction makes unclear the legal standing of the previous sections when they are being enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement, together with the Purchase Agreement and the Transaction Documents, contains the entire understanding among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations and understandings among the Buyer and Seller with respect hereto. This Agreement may be amended or modified verbally between parties with mutual agreement and record of changes.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the agreement can only be amended or modified by a written instrument signed by both parties. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement can be amended or modified verbally if both parties agree and a record of changes is kept. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about the process of modifying the contract, which is the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating that the contradiction is introduced within the same section of the document.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Notices. Any notice or other communication required or permitted to be delivered to any party shall be in writing and shall be deemed properly delivered, given and received when delivered, by hand, by registered mail, by courier or express delivery service, by facsimile, or by e-mail to the address or facsimile number set forth beneath the name of such party below, or to such other address or facsimile number as such party shall have specified in a written notice given to the other parties
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Notices. Any notice or other communication required or permitted to be delivered to any party shall be in writing; however, parties can use oral communication which will be deemed as delivered when acknowledged by receiver.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly requires that all notices be written. The modified text allows for oral communications to also serve as valid notice, creating a direct contradiction. This introduces confusion about the formal notice requirements in the agreement, particularly with legal ramifications tied to notice. Since legal documents are being exchanged, the written format makes a reference for the future when dealing with disputes; making that into a verbal agreement opens up lots of opportunities for uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any notice or other communication required or permitted to be delivered to any party shall be in writing; however, parties can use oral communication which will be deemed as delivered when acknowledged by receiver.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that all notices must be in writing. The changed text introduces the possibility of oral communication being deemed as delivered, directly contradicting the initial requirement for written notice. This creates a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text as both sections now give conflicting guidance on acceptable forms of notice, potentially leading to confusion and disputes about the validity of communicated notices.
